edge: The abstract discusses a diffusion prior derived from off-the-shelf de-noising diffusion generative models.
Efforts to adapt diffusion models for noisy inverse problems often yield unsatisfactory outcomes or require extensive iterations.
This work introduces the DiffECC method as a novel solution for image restoration.
DiffECC employs two strategies: error contraction during posterior sampling to improve restoration quality.
The first strategy combines CNNs to ensure data consistency from the start.
A restart sampling algorithm is designed to enhance the error contraction effect.
The method includes an iterative error correction for both data and prior terms within the diffusion sampling framework.
gaussian deblurring
Experimental results show that DiffECC outperforms existing sampling-based diffusion models in image reconstruction.
